notes:
dạ thưa = “respectfully” — used to politely address someone before you speak, like saying “sir/ma’am” at the start of a sentence.

It’s a polite opener, not the main message. You say it when talking to someone older, higher in status, or in formal situations.  
It shows humility and respect; without it, your sentence can sound too direct.

Structure: dạ thưa + title/pronoun (Dạ thưa cô…)

Related: thưa (formal “respectfully say”), dạ (a polite yes/attention word, especially in the South). Using both together sounds extra respectful.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Respectfully teacher, I have finished my homework.
Respectfully uncle, may I come into the house.
Respectfully brother, I will send you the documents today.
exampleSentences: Dạ thưa cô, em đã hoàn thành bài tập về nhà rồi ạ.
Dạ thưa bác, cháu xin phép vào nhà ạ.
Dạ thưa anh, em sẽ gửi tài liệu cho anh trong hôm nay.
